question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between a reversible change and an irreversible change?

options.label: A
options.text: A reversible change happens quickly, while an irreversible change happens slowly

options.label: B
options.text: A reversible change can be undone to recover the original substance, while an irreversible change cannot be easily undone

options.label: C
options.text: A reversible change involves heating, while an irreversible change involves cooling

options.label: D
options.text: A reversible change is a physical change, while an irreversible change is always a chemical change

correct_answer: A reversible change can be undone to recover the original substance, while an irreversible change cannot be easily undone
explanation: The key difference is whether the original substance can be recovered. Reversible changes like melting and freezing can be undone, but irreversible changes like burning create new substances that cannot be easily reversed.
